sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: There is still no adequate (1) ______ (EXPLAIN) why, several thousand years ago, cultures in both Egypt and South America saw the (2) ______ (INTRODUCE) of pyramids.

Was it coincidence? Many (3) ______ (SCIENCE) believe that it was, and state that there is no (4) ______ (POSSIBLE) at all that people from two distant continents traded information on (5) ______ (BUILD) techniques.

However, some (6) ______ (REVOLUTION) theories in recent years, based on the (7) ______ (DISCOVER) that the two cultures also share key myths, legends and beliefs, suggest that the (8) ______ (APPEAR) of pyramids on opposite sides of the world was no coincidence at all.
One (9) ______ (RESEARCH) working on this, Crystal Davis, said: 'The (10) ______ (IMPORTANT) of finding out the truth here cannot be exaggerated.'
11 The fax machine has broken
items.match: down, so I'm afraid you'll have to wait until tomorrow.

items.sentence: 12 A number of experiments have been carried
items.match: out by physicists which prove that time can speed up and slow down.

items.sentence: 13 Whoever came
items.match: up with that idea should have won the Nobel Prize.

items.sentence: 14 The electricity was cut
items.match: off for over three hours yesterday.

items.sentence: 15 I'd like to find
items.match: out much more about technology in the Bronze Age.

items.sentence: 16 This chemical gives
items.match: off a very nasty smell when it's heated.

items.sentence: 17 Cancer treatments have come
18 I wonder if you could put me
items.match: through to the Radiology Department, please.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
I can't wait until we do some experiments in the science lab.
forward
I'm really ______ some experiments in the science lab.

Communication changed completely when the telephone was introduced.
with
Communication changed completely ______ the telephone.

Dr Frankenstein was finally successful in bringing the monster to life.
managed
Dr Frankenstein ______ the monster to life.

I can't understand how they built the Pyramids.
impossible
I ______ how they built the Pyramids.

Fleming discovered penicillin as a result of a little luck.
resulted
A little luck ______ penicillin.

People sometimes call nurses 'angels'.
known
Nurses ______ 'angels'.

Einstein eventually realised that he was wrong.
conclusion
Einstein eventually ______ that he was wrong.

Our physics teacher tried to explain the Theory of Relativity.
attempt
Our physics teacher ______ explaining the Theory of Relativity.

We'll save enough money and then we'll buy a computer.
soon
We'll buy a computer ______ saved enough money.
This time next week, we ______ the chemistry exam.
When I grow up, ______ an inventor.
Shirley will ______ her research for the next few weeks.
Next year, Sam ______ patients at this hospital for twenty-five years.
'What is it?' 'We won't know until we ______ at it under a microscope.'
You won't get any radio reception while ______ through the tunnel.
'I'm going to set up the equipment in a minute.' '______ give you a hand?'
Do you know who ______ the fact that sound travels in waves?
I'm thinking of getting a fax ______.
The ______ of the outbreak of the disease is still unknown.
Ten million text messages are sent on ______ every minute.
Once they've finished the extension to the ______, there'll be room for them to take on a lot more workers.
Shannon doesn't dye her hair; it's ______ blonde.
